mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.09% indicates deep accommodation
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero lower bound
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 6.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, causing equity investors to reduce risk exposure and sell equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and recessionary pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 6.5%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns becomes insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate while unemployment remains elevated, indicating weakening consumer demand and profit margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 540915M and UNRATE at 6.7% suggest demand weakness
regime_hint: late-cycle demand deterioration with persistent labor slack
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of co-triggered instances, or the joint effect of RSAFS and UNRATE on forward returns is not statistically significant (interaction p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced corporate output capacity, which constrains earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 105.7244 provides baseline for decline detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances following INDPRO declines, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline sharply (month-over-month drop > 5%) while real GDP growth remains below 2% annualized, indicating construction sector weakness and reduced multiplier effects on aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change < -5% AND GDPC1 annualized growth < 2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1547K and GDPC1 at 18596.5B provide baseline
regime_hint: demand-side recession with construction sector leading decline
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect between HOUST and GDPC1 is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates to below 50K new jobs per month while inflation remains above 3%, signaling stagflationary pressure that compresses real wages and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 50K AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142624K and CPIAUCSL at 261.775 provide baseline
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of co-triggered instances, or the stagflation interaction term has Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL declines month-over-month by more than 0.3%, signaling disinflationary pressure that allows the Federal Reserve to maintain or reduce rates, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current CPIAUCSL at 261.775 provides baseline for deflation detection
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances following CPIAUCSL declines, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth accelerates above 3% annualized while retail sales growth exceeds 3% annualized, signaling robust demand expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 3% AND RSAFS annualized growth > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18596.5B and RSAFS at 540915M provide baseline for growth acceleration
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of co-triggered instances, or the joint demand expansion effect has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
